subject: Physics
title: Event Horizons Explained
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Event Horizons Explained.

hook.text: The event horizon is a black hole's point of no return. Cross it and nothing gets out—ever.

buildup.text: At this boundary, escape velocity equals the speed of light. Since nothing exceeds light, nothing escapes.

discovery.text: From outside, an object falling in appears to slow down and freeze at the horizon. It never crosses.

twist.text: From the falling person's view, nothing special happens at the horizon. They sail right through.

climax.text: This disagreement between observers is one of the deepest puzzles in modern physics.

punchline.text: One boundary. Two stories. Relativity at its strangest.

quiz.question: What happens at a black hole's event horizon?
options: Escape velocity equals the speed of light
